perturbation.original_text: At the written request of the University or upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, Recipient will, on the direction of the University, return or destroy the Information and will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of any part of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: At the written request of the University, Recipient will, on the direction of the University, return or destroy the Information; however, upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, the Recipient has the option to retain one archival copy of the Information for internal record-keeping purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of all Information and prohibits retaining any copies. The altered text permits retaining 'one archival copy'. This creates a direct contradiction, as the obligation to destroy all information is now optional under specific circumstances. This contradiction will impact the clause about Non-Disclosure since the Recipient will have an Archival Copy which they can use to disclose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return or Destruction of Information. At the written request of the University, Recipient will, on the direction of the University, return or destroy the Information; however, upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, the Recipient has the option to retain one archival copy of the Information for internal record-keeping purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Recipient will return or destroy ALL Information and will NOT retain any copy. The Changed Text states the Recipient has the OPTION to retain one archival copy. The Contradicted Text mirrors the Changed Text by stating the Recipient has the option to retain one archival copy, which contradicts the original version that forbids retention of any kind. Therefore, this creates a direct contradiction as the obligation to destroy all information is now optional, leading to an in-text contradiction based on the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: No provision of this Agreement will be deemed waived or any breach excused, unless such waiver or consent excusing the breach is in writing and signed by the University.
perturbation.changed_text: No provision of this Agreement will be deemed waived or any breach excused, unless such waiver or consent excusing the breach is in writing. The University's conduct, including but not limited to accepting late performance or failing to enforce a provision, may be construed as a waiver of that provision.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that a waiver must be explicit and in writing. The addition allows for implied waivers based on the University's conduct, contradicting the requirement for a written waiver. This creates uncertainty, making the written waiver clause less enforceable, because of the new exception regarding the University's conduct.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Waiver. No provision of this Agreement will be deemed waived or any breach excused, unless such waiver or consent excusing the breach is in writing. The University's conduct, including but not limited to accepting late performance or failing to enforce a provision, may be construed as a waiver of that provision.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that a waiver must be explicit and in writing. The changed text adds that the University's conduct may be construed as a waiver, even if not in writing. This directly contradicts the requirement for a written waiver, creating uncertainty about how waivers are valid. The contradicted text is an exact copy of the changed text which has the contradiction, meaning the contradictory text is already present in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, negotiations, agreements, understandings, representations and warranties of any form or nature, whether oral or written, and whether express or implied, which may have been entered into between the parties relating to its subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, negotiations, agreements, understandings, representations and warranties of any form or nature, whether oral or written, and whether express or implied, which may have been entered into between the parties relating to its subject matter. However, any side letters or amendments agreed to by both parties after the effective date of this agreement will be considered part of this agreement, even if not formally integrated.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause is an 'entire agreement' clause, designed to prevent reliance on agreements outside the written contract. The added sentence stating that side letters or amendments are part of the agreement contradicts this, creating uncertainty about what constitutes 'the entire agreement'. This will create contradictions on which version is final and enforcable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement and Counterpart. This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, negotiations, agreements, understandings, representations and warranties of any form or nature, whether oral or written, and whether express or implied, which may have been entered into between the parties relating to its subject matter. However, any side letters or amendments agreed to by both parties after the effective date of this agreement will be considered part of this agreement, even if not formally integrated.
perturbation.justification: The original text establishes an 'entire agreement' clause. The changed text adds a sentence stating that side letters or amendments agreed to after the effective date are also part of the agreement, even if not formally integrated. This directly contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause by introducing ambiguity about which documents constitute the entire agreement. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, meaning it contains conflicting statements. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as it introduces uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement will be governed by and construct under the laws of British Columbia and the applicable laws of Canada without reference to its conflict of law rules. Any action or proceeding brought to enforce the terms of this Agreement will be brought in a court in Vancouver, British Columbia, and the parties hereby consent and submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of such court.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be governed by and construct under the laws of British Columbia and the applicable laws of Canada without reference to its conflict of law rules. Any dispute arising out of or relating to this agreement may be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the British Columbia International Commercial Arbitration Centre, instead of court litigation. If arbitration is elected by either party, the decision of the arbitrator shall be final and binding.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates litigation in Vancouver courts. The modification introduces the possibility of binding arbitration as an alternative dispute resolution method. This creates an internal contradiction regarding whether disputes must be resolved in court or can be resolved through arbitration, affecting the jurisdiction and enforcement mechanisms. There is uncertainty on which version of enforcing the contract will be in place
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law and Jurisdiction. This Agreement will be governed by and construct under the laws of British Columbia and the applicable laws of Canada without reference to its conflict of law rules. Any dispute arising out of or relating to this agreement may be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the British Columbia International Commercial Arbitration Centre, instead of court litigation. If arbitration is elected by either party, the decision of the arbitrator shall be final and binding.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that disputes will be settled in a Vancouver court. The changed text introduces the possibility of binding arbitration as an alternative to court litigation. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, and it clearly outlines the optionality of using arbitration rather than court litigation. This creates an in-text contradiction as it's unclear whether disputes must be resolved in court, or whether arbitration is a valid option. The explanation provided confirms that the introduction of arbitration as an option contradicts the original mandate for court litigation, satisfying the condition for a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
